title: श्री राम पञ्चकम्
devanagariText:
कञ्जाताक्षं कञ्जवक्त्रं कञ्जपादमनन्यकम्।
कञ्जनाभं कुन्दहासं रामं राजीवलोचनम्॥१॥

रामं सुग्रीवमित्रं च रामं दशरथात्मजम्।
रामं सीतापतिं श्रेष्ठं रामचन्द्रं नमाम्यहम्॥२॥

कोदण्डपाणिमाजानुबाहुमीषत्स्मितोज्ज्वलम्।
पीताम्बरधरं रामं सेव्यं सर्वसुरासुरैः॥३॥

रामं रघुकुलश्रेष्ठं धर्मज्ञं सत्यवादिनम्।
एकपत्नीव्रतधरं रामं सत्पुरुषोत्तमम्॥४॥

रामं लक्ष्मणसंयुक्तं सीतासमेतमव्ययम्।
हनुमत्सेवितं देवं प्रणमामि रघूत्तमम्॥५॥

रामपञ्चकमेतत्तु यः पठेत् सततं नरः।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो रामसायुज्यमाप्नुयात्॥

॥ इति श्री राम पञ्चकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
Kanjataksham Kanjavaktram Kanjapadam Ananyakam.
Kanjanabham Kundahasam Ramam Rajivalochanam.||1||

Ramam Sugriva-Mitram Cha Ramam Dasharathatmajam.
Ramam Sitapatim Shreshtham Ramachandram Namamyaham.||2||

Kodandapanim Ajanu-Bahum Ishat-Smitojjvalam.
Pitambaradharam Ramam Sevyam Sarvasurasuraih.||3||

Ramam Raghukula-Shreshtham Dharmajnam Satyavadinam.
Ekapatni-Vratadharam Ramam Satpurushottamam.||4||

Ramam Lakshmana-Samyuktam Sita-Sametam Avyayam.
Hanumat-Sevitam Devam Pranamami Raghuttamam.||5||

Rama-Panchakam Etat Tu Yah Pathet Satatam Narah.
Sarvapapa-Vinirmukto Rama-Sayujyam Apnuyat.

|| Iti Shri Rama Panchakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
कमलनयन, कमलवदन, कमलपाद, अनन्य, कमलनाभ, कुन्द सी मुस्कान वाले, राजीवलोचन राम।

सुग्रीव मित्र राम, दशरथ आत्मज राम, सीतापति श्रेष्ठ रामचन्द्र — मैं नमन करता हूँ।

कोदण्ड (धनुष) हाथ में, जानु तक लम्बी भुजाएँ, ईषत् (हल्की) मुस्कान से उज्ज्वल, पीताम्बरधारी राम — देव-असुर सबके सेव्य।

रघुकुल श्रेष्ठ, धर्मज्ञ, सत्यवादी, एकपत्नी व्रतधारी राम — सत्पुरुषोत्तम।

लक्ष्मण से संयुक्त, सीता समेत, अव्यय, हनुमान द्वारा सेवित देव — रघूत्तम को मैं प्रणाम करता हूँ।

जो मनुष्य सतत यह राम पञ्चक पढ़ता है, वह सर्व पापों से मुक्त होकर राम सायुज्य प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Concise five-verse prayer capturing the essence of Lord Rama
Easy to memorize for daily morning recitation
Invokes all blessings of rama devotion in a short form
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and leads to rama sayujya
Invokes Rama with Sita, Lakshmana, and Hanuman
Ideal for those who want a brief but complete Rama prayer
description: Dedicated to Lord Rama, the Rama Panchakam (श्री राम पञ्चकम्) is a set of five devotional verses revered in Hindu tradition. This sacred composition is sourced from Traditional Sanskrit Stotra and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Regular recitation is believed to concise five-verse prayer capturing the essence of Lord Rama, grant all blessings of Rama devotion in a short form, and destroy all sins and leads to Rama Sayujya. This prayer is traditionally recited on Sunday (Ravivar) and during Ram Navami, Dussehra, and Vivah Panchami to invoke divine grace. With 5 verses and a reading time of about 3 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Rama Panchakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Rama Panchakam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Five beautiful verses praising Lord Rama's divine form, virtues, and grace for daily devotion.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Rama Panchakam and how many verses does it contain?
seoFaqs.answer: The Rama Panchakam (श्री राम पञ्चकम् — pancha = five, kam = collection) is a concise five-verse devotional stotra in praise of Lord Rama, preserved in the classical Sanskrit stotra tradition. Unlike the longer Rama Ashtakam (8 verses), Rama Pancharatnam (5 verses with elaborate Sanskrit metres), or the massive Rama Sahasranama (1000 names), the Rama Panchakam is deliberately short and easy to memorise — making it the ideal daily Rama prayer for busy devotees who want a complete but brief upasana. The five verses respectively praise Rama's divine form (lotus-features), his identity (friend of Sugriva, son of Dasharatha, husband of Sita), his iconic posture (with bow Kodanda and long arms), his moral character (truthful, ekapatni-vrata), and his complete divine entourage (with Lakshmana, Sita, and Hanuman). (Sources: Traditional Sanskrit stotra-sangraha.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Kanjataksham Kanjavaktram Kanjapadam?
seoFaqs.answer: This celebrated opening verse of the Rama Panchakam describes Sri Rama through five lotus epithets in a single shloka: Kanjataksham — with eyes (aksha) like the lotus (kanja); Kanjavaktram — with face (vaktra) like the lotus; Kanjapadam — with feet (pada) like the lotus; Kanjanabham — with navel (nabha) like the lotus (echoing the Kanjanabha epithet of Vishnu, source of Brahma's lotus); Ananyakam — the unparalleled, second to none. Kunda-hasam — with a smile bright as the kunda (jasmine) flower. Ramam Rajiva-lochanam — Rama, the lotus-eyed. The lotus (kanja, padma, rajiva, pankaja are all synonyms) is the supreme symbol of divine beauty rising untouched above the waters of samsara — every part of Rama's form is compared to it, making this verse one of the most beloved Rama dhyana shlokas. (Sources: Rama Panchakam verse 1; classical Vaishnava dhyana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Rama Panchakam and Rama Pancharatnam?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are five-verse Rama stotras but differ in form, source and depth: (1) the Rama Panchakam is a simpler, shorter five-verse hymn preserved in traditional Sanskrit stotra-sangraha, in straightforward Anushtup metre, focused on Rama's iconic identity (lotus-form, friend of Sugriva, son of Dasharatha, with bow Kodanda, with Sita-Lakshmana-Hanuman); each verse is independent and easily memorable, ideal for beginners and daily quick recitation; (2) the Rama Pancharatnam (राम पञ्चरत्नम् — five jewels) is a more sophisticated composition attributed to Adi Shankaracharya, in elaborate Indravajra-Upajati type metres, focused on Rama's transcendental nature (Kaivalya-natha, Sri-kantha-chapa-priya-bhanjana, Dashagriva-mardana), each verse a rasa-jewel; it is more philosophical and aimed at advanced sadhakas seeking moksha. In daily practice, Panchakam is the morning prayer, Pancharatnam the meditation. (Sources: classical stotra tradition; Adi Shankaracharya stotra-sangraha.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Ekapatni-vratadharam Ramam Satpurushottamam?
seoFaqs.answer: This celebrated fourth verse of the Rama Panchakam praises Rama as the supreme moral exemplar: Ramam Raghukula-shreshtham — Rama, the foremost of the Raghu lineage; Dharmajnam — knower of dharma; Satya-vadinam — the speaker of only truth; Ekapatni-vratadharam — the bearer of the vow of one wife (an unprecedented vow for a king of his era when polygamy was the royal norm); Ramam Satpurushottamam — Rama, the supreme among virtuous men, the very ideal of purushottama. The Ekapatni-vrata is among Rama's most revered virtues — even after Sita's exile, when he had to perform the Ashvamedha yajna requiring a wife by his side, Rama refused to remarry and instead used a golden statue of Sita as his consort. This single verse establishes Rama as the dharmic role model for all householders. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana Uttara Kanda; classical Rama upasana.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Rama Panchakam most auspicious to recite?
seoFaqs.answer: The Rama Panchakam is most powerful when recited on: (1) every Sunday (Ravivar — sacred to the Raghu-vamsha founded by Suryavansha solar dynasty, Rama being its supreme descendant); (2) every Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Thursday (Guruvar) for additional Rama-bhakti; (3) Ram Navami (Chaitra Shukla Navami — Rama's appearance day); (4) Vivah Panchami (Margashirsha Shukla Panchami — the auspicious Rama-Sita marriage day, especially celebrated at Mithila / Janakpur and Ayodhya); (5) Dussehra / Vijayadashami (Ashvin Shukla Dashami); (6) during the daily morning Sandhya as a brief Rama-smarana; (7) before commencing Ramayana Masa Parayana or Sundara Kanda parayana; (8) at the time of pilgrimage to Ayodhya, Chitrakoot, Panchavati, Hampi (Kishkindha), Rameshwaram or any Rama-tirtha; (9) for those who want a brief but complete Rama prayer when time is limited. (Sources: Drikpanchang Rama calendar; classical Rama upasana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the phalashruti of the Rama Panchakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti verse declares: Rama-panchakam etat tu yah pathet satatam narah, sarvapapa-vinirmukto Rama-sayujyam apnuyat — Whoever continually recites this Rama Panchakam becomes free from all sins and attains Rama-sayujya (merger with Rama / the supreme abode of Rama). Additional traditional phala include: complete protection by Hanuman who is invoked in the fifth verse; family harmony through invocation of Rama with Sita-Lakshmana; victory over inner enemies (kama, krodha, lobha) by smaran of Ravanari; success in righteous undertakings through Rama's grace; eligibility for darshan of Sri Rama in dream and meditation; ultimate liberation through Rama-sayujya which the Bhagavata Purana and Rama Rahasya Upanishad describe as the supreme moksha — eternal residence at Rama's feet in Saketa-loka, his transcendental abode. (Sources: Rama Panchakam phalashruti; Rama Rahasya Upanishad.)
